hypothesis: Foundation models pre-trained on SMILES representations will exhibit systematically degraded performance when predicting electrolyte properties in multi-component systems where non-covalent intermolecular interactions (hydrogen bonding, π-π stacking, ion pairing) dominate over covalent structural features, because their molecular-level embeddings lack explicit encoding of three-dimensional spatial arrangements and dynamic solvation shell geometries that emerge only at the formulation scale.
